scope_note: QA Octave Orbit Permutation cert [402]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1993) 'Pythagorean Arithmetic' Vols I-III (BEDA framework; octave transform; digital-root arithmetic); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI: doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods; orbit structure pi(3)=8, pi(9)=24); Dale (2026) 'A Complete Classification of Pythagorean Triples via Generalized Fibonacci Sequences and Pisano Periods' (Five Families paper; Table 1 = 9x9 classification, path: papers/ready-for-submission/pythagorean-five-families/pythagorean_five_families_paper.tex). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): the digital-root octave map sigma: (b,e) -> (dr(2e), b) (with dr(n)=((n-1)%9)+1) is a permutation of {1,...,9}^2 with cycle type (1, 4^2, 12^6) and order 12; sigma maps each QA orbit class to itself (Cosmos->Cosmos, Satellite->Satellite, Singularity fixed). Derivation: sigma = left-multiply by [[0,2],[1,0]] modulo 9 (1-indexed); det=−2, so sigma is a bijection; orbit preservation follows from the fact that membership in {N,T,FLP} is determined by divisibility by 3, and sigma preserves this (dr(2e) divisible by 3 iff e divisible by 3, since 2 is invertible mod 9). Cycle type: Singularity (9,9) is fixed (dr(2*9)=9, so sigma(9,9)=(9,9)); Satellite 8 pairs split into 2 four-cycles: {(3,3),(6,3),(6,6),(3,6)} and {(6,9),(9,6),(3,9),(9,3)}; Cosmos 72 pairs split into 6 twelve-cycles. Order(sigma) = lcm(1,4,12)=12; verified sigma^k != identity for k in {1,2,3,4,6}. Mirroring: cycle type (1,4^2,12^6) mirrors orbit sizes (1,8,72) = (1,2*4,6*12). Theorem NT: dr(n)=((n-1)%9)+1 is pure integer mod-9 map; sigma is pure integer; no floats. Extends cert [401] (Octave Transformation: (b,e,d,a)->(2e,b,a,2d)); extends cert [398] (Five Families partition = Table 1). The permutation sigma is the digital-root shadow of the octave transform; it is not the same as the Pisano T-step. 5 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-12.
